title: Deddf Partneriaeth Gymdeithasol a Chaffael Cyhoeddus (Cymru) 2023 / Social Partnership and Public Procurement (Wales) Act 2023
short: Deddf Partneriaeth Gymdeithasol / Social Partnership Act 2023
type: Primary Legislation — Senedd Cymru / Welsh Parliament
chapters: 49 adran / sections mewn 4 Rhan / in 4 Parts + 2 Atodlen / Schedules. Deddfwriaeth arloesol / Pioneering legislation — the first UK Act to embed social partnership, fair work, and socially responsible procurement in statute. TEMPORAL STATUS: Commenced in stages through 4 Commencement Orders. The FINAL remaining provisions commenced 25 MARCH 2026 (Commencement No. 4 Order 2025). Reporting duties commence 1 APRIL 2026. The Act is NOW SUBSTANTIALLY IN FORCE. This is the most temporally immediate instrument in the entire KL estate.
bilingualNote: Mae'r Ddeddf hon ar gael yn Gymraeg ac yn Saesneg. / This Act is available in Welsh and English. All section references are identical in both language versions. The Welsh text has equal legal standing — it is not a translation but a co-equal original. Eileen should be prepared to reference the Welsh title and key terms when advising Welsh-speaking clients or Welsh public bodies.

parliamentaryOrigin.bill: Bil Partneriaeth Gymdeithasol a Chaffael Cyhoeddus (Cymru) / Social Partnership and Public Procurement (Wales) Bill
parliamentaryOrigin.introducedBy: Hannah Blythyn AS/MS, Dirprwy Weinidog Partneriaeth Gymdeithasol / Deputy Minister for Social Partnership, 7 Mehefin / June 2022
parliamentaryOrigin.commencementDate: Various — 4 Commencement Orders, final provisions 25 March 2026
parliamentaryOrigin.commencementNotes: Commencement No. 1 (SI 2024/183): Part 1 (Social Partnership Council) — 1 April 2024. Commencement No. 2 (SI 2024/908): Part 2 (Social Partnership Duty, fair work amendment to WFG Act) — 1 October 2024. Commencement No. 3 (SI 2025/338): procurement sub-group, regulations powers — 21 March 2025 / 30 June 2025. Commencement No. 4 (SI 2025/XXX): REMAINING PROVISIONS including socially responsible procurement duties, social public works and workforce clauses, contract management duties — 25 MARCH 2026. Reporting duties — 1 APRIL 2026.

precedingDocuments.title: Comisiwn Gwaith Teg Cymru / Fair Work Commission Wales — Report (2019)
precedingDocuments.significance: The independent commission that recommended placing social partnership on a statutory footing and defined fair work as: 'workers are fairly rewarded, heard and represented, secure and able to progress in a healthy, inclusive environment where rights are respected.' This definition is embedded in the Act.

precedingDocuments.title: Deddf Llesiant Cenedlaethau'r Dyfodol (Cymru) 2015 / Well-being of Future Generations (Wales) Act 2015
precedingDocuments.significance: The foundational statute. The SPPP Act amends the WFG Act's 'Prosperous Wales' goal by substituting 'fair work' for 'decent work'. The Social Partnership Duty is exercised through the well-being objectives framework established by the WFG Act. The two Acts are INSEPARABLE.

precedingDocuments.title: Rhaglen Lywodraethu / Programme for Government (2021)
precedingDocuments.significance: The Welsh Government's Programme for Government committed to placing social partnership on a statutory footing. This Act delivers that commitment.

leadingCases.name: No reported case law — the Act's procurement provisions commenced 4 days ago (25 March 2026)
leadingCases.principle: The socially responsible procurement duties are too new for case law to have developed. SURVEILLANCE: Monitor for the first judicial review challenges to procurement decisions under the Act, the first compliance notices from the Future Generations Commissioner, and any challenges to the application of social public works or workforce clauses.
leadingCases.significance: The first disputes will likely arise from: (a) contractors challenging the inclusion of social clauses as disproportionate, (b) contracting authorities failing to follow the 'comply or explain' notification process, (c) trade unions challenging the adequacy of consensus-seeking under the Social Partnership Duty.

parts.title: Rhan 1 / Part 1 — Cyngor Partneriaeth Gymdeithasol / Social Partnership Council (ss.1-10)

sections.title: Y Cyngor / The Council — composition, role, and employer/worker voice in Welsh governance
sections.summary: Part 1 establishes the Social Partnership Council (Cyngor Partneriaeth Gymdeithasol) — a statutory tripartite body bringing together Welsh Ministers, employer representatives, and worker representatives (primarily trade unions). This is the institutional heart of the Act: a permanent forum for social dialogue on Welsh public policy. The Council advises Welsh Ministers when they set their well-being objectives under the WFG Act 2015.
sections.text:
Y CYNGOR PARTNERIAETH GYMDEITHASOL / THE SOCIAL PARTNERSHIP COUNCIL:

(1) ESTABLISHMENT (s.1):
The Welsh Ministers must establish and maintain the Social Partnership Council. It is a PERMANENT statutory body — not a temporary advisory group.

(2) COMPOSITION (s.2):
(a) Welsh Ministers (or their representatives).
(b) Persons representing the INTERESTS OF WORKERS in Wales — in practice, trade union representatives nominated by Wales TUC (Cyngor Undebau Llafur Cymru).
(c) Persons representing the INTERESTS OF EMPLOYERS in Wales.
(d) Other persons the Welsh Ministers consider appropriate.
The membership must reflect the diversity of the Welsh workforce and include representation from different sectors.

(3) FUNCTIONS (ss.3-5):
(a) ADVISING Welsh Ministers on delivering well-being objectives.
(b) Providing information and advice on matters relating to social partnership and fair work.
(c) Making recommendations to Welsh Ministers.
(d) The Welsh Ministers MUST consult the Council when setting or revising their well-being objectives under the WFG Act 2015 s.3(2)(b).

(4) PROCUREMENT SUB-GROUP (s.9-10, commenced 30 June 2025):
A specific sub-group within the Council dedicated to PUBLIC PROCUREMENT. Functions:
(a) Providing advice and information to the Council about contracting authorities' socially responsible procurement duties.
(b) Monitoring and reporting on the implementation of socially responsible procurement across Welsh public bodies.

WHAT THIS MEANS FOR EMPLOYERS:

For Welsh PUBLIC BODIES: you have a statutory forum through which your workforce representatives will influence your well-being objectives. This is not a box-ticking exercise — the Council has genuine advisory authority.

For EMPLOYERS who contract with Welsh public bodies: the Council and its procurement sub-group monitor how public procurement supports fair work. Your employment practices may be scrutinised as part of this oversight.

For TRADE UNIONS: statutory recognition of the worker voice in Welsh governance. This is the most advanced social partnership framework in the UK.
sections.keyPrinciple: The Social Partnership Council institutionalises tripartite dialogue in Wales. Unlike the UK-level framework (where social partnership is informal), the Welsh framework is STATUTORY — the Council exists in law, has defined functions, and Welsh Ministers must consult it. This represents a fundamentally different approach to industrial relations governance from England.
refs: Wales TUC / Cyngor Undebau Llafur Cymru
commonErrors: Treating the Social Partnership Council as purely advisory with no practical impact — the Welsh Ministers MUST consult it when setting well-being objectives. The Council's views directly influence Welsh Government priorities, which in turn shape procurement decisions and public body obligations

Not understanding that the Council includes EMPLOYER representatives — this is not a trade union body. It is a tripartite forum. Employers operating in Wales should engage with employer representation on the Council

parts.title: Rhan 2 / Part 2 — Dyletswydd Partneriaeth Gymdeithasol a Gwaith Teg / Social Partnership Duty and Fair Work (ss.11-18)

sections.title: Y ddyletswydd i geisio consensws / The duty to seek consensus with trade unions, and the statutory definition of fair work
sections.summary: Part 2 imposes a SOCIAL PARTNERSHIP DUTY on all public bodies listed in the WFG Act 2015 s.6. When setting their well-being objectives, these bodies must SEEK CONSENSUS OR COMPROMISE with their recognised trade unions (or, where no union is recognised, other staff representatives). Part 2 also amends the WFG Act's 'Prosperous Wales' goal by substituting 'FAIR WORK' for 'decent work' — embedding fair work as a statutory well-being goal for Wales.
sections.text:
Y DDYLETSWYDD PARTNERIAETH GYMDEITHASOL / THE SOCIAL PARTNERSHIP DUTY (s.13):

Every public body listed in the WFG Act 2015 s.6 must, when setting well-being objectives:

(a) SEEK CONSENSUS with their recognised trade unions.
(b) Where consensus cannot be reached, seek COMPROMISE.
(c) Where no trade union is recognised, consult other representatives of their staff.
(d) Have regard to the importance of involving workers in decisions that affect them.

What 'seek consensus or compromise' means in practice:
— The body must GENUINELY ENGAGE — not present a finished plan and ask for rubber-stamp approval.
— The body must provide sufficient information for the trade unions/representatives to make informed contributions.
— The body must consider the representations made and explain how they have been taken into account.
— If consensus cannot be reached, the body must explain the points of difference and how it has sought to accommodate the union/representative position.
— This is NOT collective bargaining in the TULRCA sense — it is a broader strategic dialogue about well-being priorities.

GWAITH TEG / FAIR WORK (s.14, amending WFG Act s.4):

The Act replaces 'decent work' with 'FAIR WORK' in the description of 'Cymru Lewyrchus / A Prosperous Wales':

Original WFG Act goal: 'An innovative, productive and low carbon society which recognises the limits of the global environment and therefore uses resources efficiently and proportionately (including acting on climate change); and which develops a skilled and well-educated population in an economy which generates wealth and provides employment opportunities, allowing people to take advantage of the wealth generated through securing decent work.'

Amended: '...through securing FAIR WORK.'

Fair work (gwaith teg) is defined in the Act as:
'a range of potential activities undertaken by employers in agreement with the workforce, which contribute to well-being and improved public service delivery.'

The Fair Work Commission Wales (2019) defined it more fully:
'Workers are fairly rewarded, heard and represented, secure and able to progress in a healthy, inclusive environment where rights are respected.'

Five dimensions of fair work:
(1) FAIRLY REWARDED — fair pay, including the Real Living Wage.
(2) HEARD AND REPRESENTED — effective voice, including through trade unions.
(3) SECURE — job security, stable contracts, predictable hours.
(4) ABLE TO PROGRESS — training, development, career pathways.
(5) HEALTHY AND INCLUSIVE — safe working environment, equality, dignity.

PUBLIC BODIES SUBJECT TO THE DUTY (WFG Act s.6 list):
— Gweinidogion Cymru / Welsh Ministers
— Awdurdodau lleol / Local authorities in Wales (22)
— Byrddau Iechyd Lleol / Local Health Boards (7)
— Ymddiriedolaethau GIG / NHS Trusts (Public Health Wales, Velindre, Welsh Ambulance Service)
— Awdurdodau Tân ac Achub / Fire and rescue authorities (3)
— Cyfoeth Naturiol Cymru / Natural Resources Wales
— Cyngor Celfyddydau Cymru / Arts Council of Wales
— Chwaraeon Cymru / Sport Wales
— Llyfrgell Genedlaethol Cymru / National Library of Wales
— Amgueddfa Cymru / National Museum Wales
— Parciau Cenedlaethol / National Park authorities (3)
— And others listed in Schedule 1 of the SPPP Act

IMPACT ON EMPLOYERS:

For listed PUBLIC BODIES: the Social Partnership Duty is NOW a statutory obligation (in force since 1 October 2024). When setting or revising well-being objectives, you MUST engage with your trade unions or staff representatives in seeking consensus or compromise.

For PRIVATE SECTOR employers operating in Wales: you are not directly subject to the Social Partnership Duty. But if you CONTRACT with a Welsh public body, the fair work culture embedded by this duty will flow into procurement expectations. A supplier who does not practise fair work may find itself at a competitive disadvantage when bidding for Welsh public contracts.
sections.keyPrinciple: The Social Partnership Duty is the mechanism by which worker voice is embedded in Welsh public body governance. It is not collective bargaining — it is broader strategic dialogue about well-being priorities. But it creates a genuine obligation to engage with trade unions that goes beyond anything required in England. The fair work definition (5 dimensions) is the standard against which Welsh public body employment practices — and increasingly, their suppliers' practices — will be measured.
refs: WFG Act 2015 s.4 (as amended — 'fair work' goal)
Commencement No. 2 Order (SI 2024/908) — in force 1 October 2024
commonErrors: Treating 'seek consensus' as 'inform and consult' — it is STRONGER than information and consultation. The body must genuinely try to reach agreement with trade union representatives. Presenting a finished plan is not consensus-seeking

Not understanding that fair work is now a STATUTORY GOAL for Welsh public bodies — 'Cymru Lewyrchus / A Prosperous Wales' includes fair work as a core element. Every well-being objective set by a Welsh public body must consider how it contributes to fair work

Assuming the fair work definition is aspirational — it is the STATUTORY STANDARD against which practices will be measured. The five dimensions (fairly rewarded, heard and represented, secure, able to progress, healthy and inclusive) are not optional — they define what fair work means in Wales
Not listing all affected bodies — the duty applies to 44+ public bodies listed in the WFG Act s.6, plus additional bodies in SPPP Act Schedule 1. If you are on the list, you are subject to the duty

versions.text: IN FORCE: Social Partnership Duty (s.13) operational from 1 October 2024. Fair work amendment to WFG Act (s.14) in force. All listed public bodies must seek consensus or compromise with trade unions when setting well-being objectives.
versions.policyRationale: The Welsh Government's commitment to making Wales a 'fair work nation' — moving from policy aspiration to statutory obligation.

parts.title: Rhan 3 / Part 3 — Caffael Cyhoeddus Cymdeithasol Gyfrifol / Socially Responsible Public Procurement (ss.19-42)

sections.title: Y ddyletswydd caffael, cymalau gwaith a gweithlu, a rheoli contractau / Procurement duty, works and workforce clauses, and contract management
sections.summary: Part 3 is the operational core of the Act — and the provisions that most directly affect employers who CONTRACT with Welsh public bodies. It imposes socially responsible procurement duties on contracting authorities, requires them to consider including social public works clauses (major construction) and social public workforce clauses (outsourced services) in contracts, and creates contract management duties to monitor compliance through supply chains. Key provisions commenced 25 MARCH 2026. Reporting duties from 1 APRIL 2026.
sections.text:
CAFFAEL CYHOEDDUS CYMDEITHASOL GYFRIFOL / SOCIALLY RESPONSIBLE PROCUREMENT:

(1) THE PROCUREMENT DUTY (s.19):
Every contracting authority must carry out public procurement in a SOCIALLY RESPONSIBLE WAY. This means taking action aimed at contributing to the WFG Act well-being goals (including fair work) through procurement decisions.

(2) SOCIALLY RESPONSIBLE PROCUREMENT OBJECTIVES (s.20):
Every contracting authority must:
(a) SET socially responsible procurement objectives.
(b) PUBLISH those objectives.
(c) REVIEW AND REVISE them regularly.
The objectives must relate to the well-being goals (WFG Act s.4).

(3) DUTY TO TAKE STEPS TO MEET OBJECTIVES (s.21):
When procuring prescribed contracts, the contracting authority must take all reasonable steps to meet its socially responsible procurement objectives.

CONTRACTAU ADEILADU MAWR / MAJOR CONSTRUCTION CONTRACTS (ss.22-30):

Threshold: £2 MILLION or more (public works contract, works contract, or works concession contract).

(a) MODEL SOCIAL PUBLIC WORKS CLAUSES (s.23): Welsh Ministers must publish model clauses covering: PAYMENTS (fair payment terms, prompt payment through supply chain), EMPLOYMENT (fair work, Real Living Wage, local employment), COMPLIANCE (with employment law, H&S, environmental standards), TRAINING (apprenticeships, workforce development), SUB-CONTRACTING (fair supply chain practices), ENVIRONMENT (carbon reduction, sustainability).

(b) DUTY TO CONSIDER INCLUSION (s.24): The contracting authority must consider whether to include social public works clauses in the contract.

(c) DUTY TO TAKE REASONABLE STEPS TO INCLUDE (s.25): If the authority decides to include clauses, it must take all reasonable steps to include them.

(d) CONTRACT MANAGEMENT (s.26): The authority must take all reasonable steps to ensure clauses are implemented during the contract.

(e) SUB-CONTRACTS (s.27): The authority must take all reasonable steps to ensure social public works clauses are applied to sub-contracts.

(f) NOTIFICATION IF NOT INCLUDING (s.29): If the authority decides NOT to include clauses, it must NOTIFY WELSH MINISTERS in writing, explaining why. This is the 'COMPLY OR EXPLAIN' mechanism — the most powerful accountability tool in the Act.

CONTRACTAU ALLANOLI GWASANAETHAU / OUTSOURCING SERVICES CONTRACTS (ss.31-38):

(a) PUBLIC SERVICES OUTSOURCING AND WORKFORCE CODE (s.32): Welsh Ministers must publish a code including model workforce clauses. The code covers: terms and conditions of the transferred/outsourced workforce, trade union recognition, pension provision, training and development, pay (including Real Living Wage).

(b) SOCIAL PUBLIC WORKFORCE CLAUSES (s.33): The authority must consider whether to include social public workforce clauses.

(c) DUTY TO TAKE REASONABLE STEPS (s.34): If the authority decides to include clauses, reasonable steps to include and implement.

(d) NOTIFICATION IF NOT INCLUDING (s.35): Same 'comply or explain' mechanism as for construction contracts.

The outsourcing provisions are particularly significant for FM, cleaning, catering, security, and IT service providers to Welsh public bodies. If you outsource staff to a Welsh NHS body or local authority, your workforce practices will be subject to the workforce code and social clauses.

ADRODD / REPORTING (ss.39-42, from 1 APRIL 2026):

(a) CONTRACTING AUTHORITIES must publish annual socially responsible procurement reports for prescribed contracts.
(b) WELSH MINISTERS must produce an annual report on public procurement in Wales.
(c) Reports must include: how socially responsible procurement objectives have been met, how social clauses have been applied, where notifications (comply or explain) have been issued.

The reporting framework creates TRANSPARENCY — anyone can see which authorities are embedding fair work in procurement and which are relying on exemptions.

WHAT THIS MEANS FOR SUPPLIERS AND CONTRACTORS:

If you bid for Welsh public contracts:

(1) YOUR BID will be evaluated on fair work credentials. Do you:
— Pay the Real Living Wage (currently £12.60/hour)?
— Recognise a trade union?
— Offer secure employment (not zero-hours unless genuinely flexible)?
— Provide training and development opportunities?
— Maintain a healthy, inclusive workplace?

(2) YOUR CONTRACT may include social public works or workforce clauses requiring you to maintain these standards throughout the contract term.

(3) YOUR SUPPLY CHAIN may be monitored. Sub-contractors may need to meet the same fair work standards.

(4) YOUR REPORTING may need to demonstrate ongoing compliance.

(5) YOUR COMPETITIVE ADVANTAGE: employers who already practise fair work will have a NATURAL ADVANTAGE in Welsh public procurement. Those who do not will need to change or risk losing Welsh public sector business.

INTERACTION WITH ERA 2025:

The ERA 2025 and the SPPP Act are PARALLEL BUT COMPLEMENTARY frameworks:

— ERA 2025: UK-wide employment rights (unfair dismissal, trade union rights, fair work agency). Applies to ALL employers.
— SPPP Act: Welsh-specific procurement and social partnership duties. Applies to Welsh public bodies and their supply chains.

The two frameworks REINFORCE each other. An employer who complies with the ERA 2025 (Day-One rights, union access, enhanced protections) will be well-positioned to meet the SPPP Act's fair work requirements. But the SPPP Act goes FURTHER in some respects — for example, the Real Living Wage expectation exceeds the statutory NMW/NLW.
sections.keyPrinciple: Part 3 transforms fair work from a policy aspiration into a procurement reality. Every Welsh public body that buys goods, works, or services must now consider whether to embed fair work requirements in its contracts — and must explain to Welsh Ministers if it decides not to. For suppliers, this means that fair work is no longer a nice-to-have — it is a condition of doing business with the Welsh public sector. This is the most advanced procurement-based fair work framework in the UK.
refs: Commencement No. 4 Order 2025 — ss.19-42 in force from 25 March 2026
Welsh Government model social public works clauses
Welsh Government public services outsourcing and workforce code
Real Living Wage Foundation — current rate

regulatoryGuidance.title: Draft guidance on applying the SPPP Act's duties in practice
regulatoryGuidance.relevance: Welsh Government guidance on implementing the socially responsible procurement duties, including model clauses and reporting requirements.

commonErrors: Not knowing that the procurement provisions commenced 25 MARCH 2026 — this is the most temporally immediate obligation in the Welsh framework. Contracting authorities should ALREADY be applying these duties to new procurements

Assuming social clauses are optional — they are subject to 'comply or explain'. If the authority decides not to include them, it must NOTIFY Welsh Ministers. This creates accountability and transparency that will drive compliance

Not understanding the £2 million threshold for major construction contracts — this is a LOW threshold. Many routine public construction projects exceed £2 million and will trigger the social public works clauses requirement

Treating the outsourcing workforce code as guidance only — contracting authorities must HAVE REGARD to the code. It includes model clauses. The expectation is that all clauses will be included unless there is a proportionate reason not to

Not preparing supply chain compliance — the Act requires monitoring of social clauses through SUB-CONTRACTS. A main contractor who sub-contracts to firms that do not meet fair work standards may find itself in breach of the contract terms

Not paying the Real Living Wage when bidding for Welsh public contracts — while the NMW/NLW is the legal minimum, the SPPP Act's fair work framework expects the Real Living Wage (currently £12.60/hour). Bidders who pay only NMW may score lower on fair work criteria

Ignoring reporting duties from 1 April 2026 — contracting authorities must publish annual socially responsible procurement reports. Suppliers who cannot demonstrate compliance will be visible in these reports

versions.text: IN FORCE: socially responsible procurement duties, social public works clauses, social public workforce clauses, contract management duties. Reporting duties from 1 April 2026. The Act is NOW SUBSTANTIALLY IN FORCE.
versions.policyRationale: The Welsh Government's final commencement stage — moving from framework duties to operational procurement requirements. £7 billion per year in Welsh public procurement is now subject to socially responsible procurement obligations.

pendingAmendments.status: Annual socially responsible procurement reports commence 1 April 2026. First reports expected covering the 2026-27 financial year.
pendingAmendments.aceiImpact: HIGH — creates transparency and accountability framework

pendingAmendments.status: Welsh Government is publishing model social public works clauses and the outsourcing workforce code. Monitor for final versions and any revisions.
pendingAmendments.aceiImpact: HIGH — model clauses will define the practical obligations on contractors

pendingAmendments.instrument: First judicial review or compliance challenge
pendingAmendments.status: No case law yet — monitor for the first challenges to procurement decisions under the Act, particularly around the proportionality of social clauses.
pendingAmendments.aceiImpact: HIGH — will establish practical interpretation of the duties

pendingAmendments.instrument: ERA 2025 interaction — Fair Work Agency and Welsh fair work framework
pendingAmendments.status: Monitor for any overlap or complementarity between the UK-wide Fair Work Agency (April 2026) and the Welsh Social Partnership Council. The two frameworks operate in parallel but may interact on enforcement.
pendingAmendments.aceiImpact: MODERATE — potential for complementary or overlapping enforcement

surveillanceStatus.status: ACTIVE — procurement provisions commenced 25 March 2026, reporting from 1 April 2026
